title: Rescue mission to Vandos

{{Quote|He was relentless in his pursuit and personally killed countless troopers and Jedi.|Flynn Kybo, describing General Grievous|<ref name="gg1" />}}
The '''rescue mission to Vandos''' took place in [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. In an attempt to lure several [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] into a trap on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Vandos]], [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederate]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]] kidnapped [[Anx/Legends|Anx]] [[Ambassador/Legends|Ambassador]] [[Quiyyen]] and [[Prison/Legends|confined]] him within a dug-out cave. After learning about the kidnapping, as well as a Confederate invasion [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] arriving in the [[Vandos system/Legends|Vandos system]], the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] sent a rescue team led by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[T'chooka D'oon]] to Vandos.

During the mission, D'oon was accompanied by his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], [[Flynn Kybo]]; Jedi Master [[Jmmaar/Legends|Jmmaar]]; and a group of [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]]. As they entered the cave, Kybo learned that they had fallen into a trap as they were confronted by Grievous and his [[B2-series super battle droid/Legends|super battle droid]] force. After Kybo rescued the ambassador, D'oon's team prepared to retreat to their landed [[Gunship/Legends|gunship]] outside the cave. To give his team more [[Time/Legends|time]] to board the [[Transport/Legends|transport]], D'oon attempted to distract Grievous, though the [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] general quickly [[Death/Legends|slew]] the Jedi Master. The fallen Jedi Master's team, however, managed to escape Vandos with Quiyyen.

==Prelude==
In [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]],<ref name="nec" /> the [[Kaleesh/Legends|Kaleesh]] [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederate]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]], a known [[Jedi hunter/Legends|Jedi hunter]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], established a plan to lure several [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] into a dug-out cave on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Vandos]]. In order to attract their attention, Grievous kidnapped [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Ambassador/Legends|Ambassador]] [[Quiyyen]] and [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] him in the cave. While the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] was unaware of Grievous' trap, they learned about the kidnapping, as well as information about a Confederate invasion [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] arriving in the [[Vandos system/Legends|Vandos system]]. In order to rescue the [[Anx/Legends|Anx]] ambassador, the Jedi dispatched [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]] [[T'chooka D'oon]]<ref name="gg1" /> and [[Jmmaar/Legends|Jmmaar]];<ref name="jmmaar" /> D'oon's [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], [[Flynn Kybo]]; and a group of [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] from the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]].<ref name="gg1" /> Jmmaar and D'oon had a history with Grievous, having mediated the resolution to the [[Huk War]], which resulted in destitution for the Kaleesh people. Grievous, then known as Qymaen jal Sheelal, never forgot the actions of those Jedi.<ref name="jmmaar" />

==The mission==
{{Quote|The rescue mission to Vandos was to be simple—to land ahead of the approaching Separatist invasion fleet and bring out Ambassador Quiyyen. We found instead a large force of battle droids awaiting us. The ambassador was the ''bait'' for the trap.|Jedi Padawan Flynn Kybo, on the rescue of Quiyyen|<ref name="gg1" />}}
[[File:MissionToVandosLAAT-GG1.jpg|thumb|left|200px|D'oon's team leaving Vandos]]
Believing that there was indeed an approaching invasion fleet from the [[Confederate Navy/Legends|Confederate Navy]], D'oon's team needed to land on Vandos and rescue Ambassador Quiyyen before it arrived. Once the team landed their [[Low Altitude Assault Transport/infantry/Legends|LAAT/i]] [[Gunship/Legends|gunship]] near the cave, they left the vessel and—to their surprise—faced a group of [[B2-series super battle droid/Legends|super battle droids]]. Kybo realized that they had fallen into a trap and that Quiyyen was only the bait. Grievous, who was leading the [[Droid/Legends|droids]], told his [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] to not let the Jedi escape alive. During the onslaught, Grievous killed many [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]]<ref name="gg1" /> and Jmmaar, which he enjoyed as a form of revenge for Jmmaar's role in the Huk War.<ref name="jmmaar" />

Kybo, however, found Quiyyen and brought him safely toward the Jedi group. With the Anx ambassador under their guard, the team raced toward their gunship. As they proceeded to board their [[Transport/Legends|transport]], the super battle droids continued to [[Laser/Legends|fire]] their [[Wrist blaster/Legends|wrist blasters]] at the Jedi team. Seeing that his comrades needed more [[Time/Legends|time]] to board the gunship, D'oon turned his attention to the approaching Grievous and [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|engaged]] the cyborg general in a [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|lightsaber duel]]. Kybo watched as Grievous made short work of D'oon with his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]],<ref name="gg1" /> an event that would stay fresh in Kybo's mind.<ref name="gg4">[[General Grievous 4|''General Grievous'' 4]]</ref> With the group aboard the gunship, they departed Vandos and headed for the Republic [[Capital/Legends|capital]] planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="gg1" />

==Aftermath==
{{Dialogue|Kybo|I'm not talking about a military action. A small force of Jedi, no more than a handful, that would infiltrate and target Grievous.|Windu|You're talking about assassination.|Yoda|Beneath our honor that is. Murder it is. Cowardice I call it.|attr=Kybo confers with Mace Windu and Yoda|src=<ref name="gg1" />}}
Grievous took Jmmaar's lightsaber and carried it for the rest of the war, considering it among his favourite weapons.<ref name="Rots">[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'' novelization]]</ref> After the mission, Kybo was present in the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] and entered a meeting with the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]]. Because of the death of his master, as well as many other Jedi, Kybo wanted to seek revenge against Grievous. Instead of military action to target Grievous, Kybo asked Jedi Masters [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] and [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] if he could organize a small group of Jedi and target the cyborg general, an act that both Windu and Yoda frowned upon. Behind their backs, Kybo disobeyed their advice and sought out Jedi who were interested.<ref name="gg1" /> That action would later drive Kybo to his death, as he [[Battle of Belsus|engaged]] Grievous in a lightsaber duel on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Belsus/Legends|Belsus]], where he was killed when an explosion caught him by surprise.<ref name="gg4" />

==Behind the scenes==
The rescue mission to Vandos first appeared in the [[General Grievous 1|first issue]] of the [[Star Wars comic books|comic-book]] series ''[[Star Wars: General Grievous]]''. The issue was written by [[Chuck Dixon]], illustrated by [[Rick Leonardi]], and published by [[Dark Horse Comics]]<ref name="gg1" /> on [[March 16]], [[2005]].<ref name="Comicdate">{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/10-576/Star-Wars-General-Grievous-1-of-4|text=Star Wars General Grievous #1 (of 4)}}</ref>
